meaning: n.总督(或州长等地方行政长官)的职位(或职责、任期)
examples: The governorship went to a Democrat, Mrs. Anne Richards.
Mr Corzine spent a fortune to win first a Senate seat and then the governorship.
Richard Nixon “retired” in 1962 after losing an election for the governorship of California.
North Carolina was viewed to be the Republicans' best chance of picking up a governorship.
In Delaware, CNN projects State Treasurer and Democrat Jack Markell will win the governorship.
